title: Relapsed/refractory IgG kappa multiple myeloma with therapy-related MDS in an 81-year-old male

patient.chief_complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
patient.vital_signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)

1.setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
1.capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.

2.capabilities: The patient is undergoing further diagnostic clarification. You can organize bone marrow and tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, comprehensive staging imaging, and extended laboratory evaluations. Interdisciplinary consultations are available. Some specialized test results may still be pending.

3.setting: Specialist hematology conference (definitive diagnosis and treatment planning)
3.capabilities: You now have access to definitive pathology, cytogenetics, molecular findings, full staging, and a detailed treatment history. You are expected to integrate these data with comorbidity and transplant scores to establish the final diagnosis, risk stratification, and a realistic treatment and follow-up strategy.

demographics.valid_request_if: Requests for demographics, performance status, comorbidities, social situation, or anthropometric data.
demographics.content:
Age: 81 years
Sex: Male
Performance status: ECOG 0
HCT-CI score: 1
Comorbidities: Atrial fibrillation, arterial hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, CKD stage 3, cataract, and mild peripheral neuropathy.
Social history: The patient lives with his wife and has two adult children (ages 56 and 49). He is a retired lawyer.
Height/Weight: 183 cm, 86 kg

history_physical.valid_request_if: Requests for history of present illness, review of systems, physical examination findings, or current medications.
history_physical.content:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.

cbc_coagulation.valid_request_if: Requests for complete blood count, platelets, red cell indices, and coagulation profile.
cbc_coagulation.content:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.

chemistry_immunology.valid_request_if: Requests for chemistry, renal parameters, calcium/phosphate, LDH, uric acid, immunoglobulins, and cardiac biomarkers.
chemistry_immunology.content:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.

ct_skeleton.valid_request_if: Requests for CT skeleton or skeletal imaging findings.
ct_skeleton.content: 2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.

abdominal_ultrasound.valid_request_if: Requests for abdominal ultrasound or sonography of abdominal organs.
abdominal_ultrasound.content: Abdominal ultrasound (2025-07-03): Normal sized liver and spleen, no intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy. Both kidneys small and echogenic with loss of corticomedullary differentiation. No signs of post-renal obstruction / hydronephrosis.

echocardiogram.valid_request_if: Requests for echocardiography or cardiac function assessment.
echocardiogram.content: 2025-07-06: Echocardiogram: Atrial fibrillation with irregular ventricular response; reduced LVEF 46 %; no relevant valvular disease, no pericardial effusion.

bone_marrow.valid_request_if: Requests for bone marrow morphology, plasma-cell percentage, flow cytometry, or histology.
bone_marrow.content:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.

cytogenetics_fish.valid_request_if: Requests for cytogenetic or FISH results in plasma cells or CD138-negative fraction.
cytogenetics_fish.content:
FISH analysis revealed trisomy 3, t(4;14)(p16;q32) involving IGH::FGFR3/MMSET, deletion 13q14, deletion of CDKN2A, gain of 1q21, and deletion of 17p.
Conventional cytogenetics demonstrated a complex karyotype in the CD138-negative fraction.

neuropathy_pft.valid_request_if: Requests for peripheral neuropathy assessment or pulmonary function testing.
neuropathy_pft.content:
Peripheral neuropathy screening showed mild length-dependent sensory polyneuropathy, with reduced vibration sense at both ankles, preserved motor strength, and normal reflexes.
Pulmonary function testing: FEV₁ 81 % predicted, DLCO 74 % predicted.

infection_and_serology.valid_request_if: Requests for infection screening or viral serologies.
infection_and_serology.content:
Infection screen: CRP 1.4 mg/L, PCT <0.1 ng/mL. Blood cultures remained sterile. Nasopharyngeal swab: negative for respiratory viruses.
Viral serologies: EBV IgG positive, IgM negative; CMV IgG negative; HIV negative; HBsAg negative, HBsAb positive; HCV antibody negative.

protein_studies.valid_request_if: Requests for urine analysis, serum and urine protein electrophoresis, immunofixation, and myeloma-related markers.
protein_studies.content:
Urinalysis: proteinuria (++), leukocyturia (120/µL), positive for albumin; κ/λ free light-chain ratio 18.3.
Serum protein electrophoresis revealed a distinct monoclonal IgG κ band with an M-spike, confirmed by immunofixation. Urine electrophoresis demonstrated monoclonal κ light-chain proteinuria.
β₂-microglobulin 4.8 mg/L, serum albumin 3.2 g/dL.

pet_ct.valid_request_if: Requests for FDG PET-CT or whole-body metabolic imaging.
pet_ct.content: 2025-07-06: FDG PET-CT: Demonstration of a focal FDG-avid lesion at the right shoulder and upper arm (SUVmax 8.2), correlating with the clinical swelling. Additional hypermetabolic foci were seen in the proximal humerus (SUVmax 6.5) and right scapula (SUVmax 5.9), compatible with active myeloma involvement. No other FDG-avid lesions were detected in the skeleton, visceral organs, or lymph nodes.

molecular_panel.valid_request_if: Requests for next-generation sequencing results or mutation analysis.
molecular_panel.content: Next-generation sequencing (NGS) revealed a TP53 mutation (VAF 24%) in the CD138-negative fraction. No mutations were detected in ASXL1, DNMT3A, TET2, RUNX1, SRSF2, EZH2, IDH1, IDH2, STAG2, RAD21, or SF3B1.

mrd_status.valid_request_if: Requests for measurable residual disease status and proposed markers for follow-up.
mrd_status.content: Active disease with measurable FDG-avid lesions on PET-CT. Serum M-protein (IgG κ) and free light-chain ratio (κ/λ), alongside PET-CT for remission monitoring.

treatment_history.valid_request_if: Requests for prior myeloma treatments, transplant history, and evolution of therapy-related MDS.
treatment_history.content:
2013-03-01: Initial diagnosis of IgG κ multiple myeloma, without CRAB criteria. Staging: ISS II, Salmon & Durie II. Initial management: Watch & wait.
2014-09-01; Disease progression with anemia. Initiation of induction therapy with VCD for 3 cycles (q21d): Bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. (days 1, 4, 8, 11) , Cyclophosphamide 900 mg/m² i.v. (day 1), Dexamethasone 40 mg p.o. (days 1–4 and 9–12)
2015-09-01: Stem cell mobilization with CE regimen: Cyclophosphamide 2 g/m² i.v. (days 1–2), etoposide 100 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–3), followed by G-CSF 5 µg/kg/day from day 4 until stem cell collection.
2015-10-01: High-dose melphalan therapy 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. (total 140 mg/m²), followed by autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT). No maintenance therapy administered.
2017-01-01 Biochemical signs of relapse with slowly rising IgG (from 1300 → 1850 mg/dL over 12 months) and increasing κ free light chains (120 → 220 mg/L).
2018-02-04: Bone marrow biopsy: 20 % plasma cell infiltration. FISH: trisomy 3, t(4;14), 13q14 deletion, 1q21 gain, CDKN2A deletion. CT skeleton: no new osteolytic lesions.
2018-11-01: Slim-CRAB criteria met due to FLC ratio >100 (IgG 2312 mg/dL).
Echocardiogram: preserved systolic function (LVEF 58%), no valvular abnormalities. Worsening kidney function (creatinine 1.6 mg/dL). Start of KRD (q28d): Carfilzomib 20 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–2, cycle 1), then 27 mg/m² twice weekly (days 1–2, 8–9, 15–16). Lenalidomide 15 mg p.o. (days 1–21) Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly
2019-02-01: Serological complete remission (CR) achieved. No bone marrow reassessment performed.
2019-06-01: Second high-dose therapy with melphalan 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. followed by ASCT.
Course complicated by onset of peripheral neuropathy and severe infectious episode (neutropenic fever requiring i.v. antibiotics) as well as acute kidney failure.
2021-04-01: Biochemical relapse: Immunofixation positive again with slowly rising IgG (1200 → 1650 mg/dL) and κ free light chains (84 → 187 mg/L).
2022-02-04. Start of Isa-Pom-Dex regimen: Isatuximab 10 mg/kg i.v. weekly x4, then every 2 weeks, Pomalidomide 3 mg p.o. (days 1–21), Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly: achieved VGPR.
2022-11-01: Lymphocyte apheresis performed in preparation for CAR-T therapy.
2023-01-30: CAR-T cell therapy with idecabtagene vicleucel following lymphodepletion with: Fludarabine 30 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3, 50% dose reduction due to CKD stage 3), Cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3). Infusion on 2023-01-30. Achieved VGPR, followed by CR on 2023-03-24.
2023-07-01 Cytopenia developed without evidence of therapy-related MDS or myeloma relapse.
2024-02-01 CT scan: new osteolytic lesions. IgG 1920 mg/dL and κ free light chains 191 mg/L. Immunofixation positive. Echocardiogram: LVEF 45%. Initiated ixazomib 4 mg (days 1, 8, 15) + dexamethasone 20 mg weekly, omitting IMiD due to mild cytopenia and CKD.
2024-10-01: Progressive cytopenia. Bone marrow: dysplastic changes with erythroid and myeloid atypia, no excess blasts. TP53 mutation (high VAF) detected in CD138-negative fraction; karyotyping unsuccessful. Ixazomib monotherapy continued briefly, but paused in December 2024 resulting in increased blood counts.

disease_status.valid_request_if: Requests for a concise summary of the current disease classification.
disease_status.content: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (rrMM) with concurrent therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome with TP53 mutation (MDS-pCT).

special_considerations.valid_request_if: Requests for contextual information relevant to treatment decisions, including comorbidities, prior therapies, and functional status.
special_considerations.content: Patient with rr IgG kappa multiple myeloma initially diagnosed in 2013, who has undergone multiple lines of therapy including two autologous stem cell transplants, KRD, Isa-Pom-Dex, and CAR-T with ide-cel, achieving CR in 2023. Since 2024, disease course complicated by progressive but compensated cytopenia, dysplastic marrow changes, and detection of a cKT and TP53 mutation with high VAF in the CD138⁻ fraction, in line with therapy-related MDS. Cardiac function moderately reduced (LVEF 45%). The patient remains clinically stable.

round_1.working_diagnosis: Relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary manifestation

treatment_plan.action: Treatment of hypercalcemia and pause calcium carbonate therapy
Ultrasound/CT-guided biopsy of soft tissue swelling
Complete MM diagnostics with immunofixation and 24-h urine

key_findings_quotes: swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted
Comorbidities: Atrial fibrillation, arterial hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, CKD stage 3, cataract, and mild peripheral neuropathy

round_2.working_diagnosis: Relapsed multiple myeloma with extramedullary lesion and therapy-related MDS

treatment_plan.action: Ultrasound/CT-guided biopsy of soft tissue swelling to confirm extramedullary myeloma
Myeloid panel sequencing of CD138-negative population in BM aspirate

key_findings_quotes: nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10% of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138+ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction
FISH analysis revealed trisomy 3, t(4;14)(p16;q32) involving IGH::FGFR3/MMSET, deletion 13q14, deletion of CDKN2A, gain of 1q21, and deletion of 17p
focal FDG-avid lesion at the right shoulder and upper arm (SUVmax 8.2). No other FDG-avid lesions were detected in the skeleton, visceral organs, or lymph nodes
Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis
complex karyotype in the CD138-negative fraction

round_3.working_diagnosis: Multiple myeloma IgG kappa with FISH-defined high-risk cytogenetics (R-ISS II) and MDS-pCT with TP53 mutation
differentials: High-risk relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma with extramedullary disease

treatment_plan.action: Biopsy of the right upper arm lesion to confirm extramedullary myeloma involvement and BCMA expression
Initiate anti-MM therapy with talquetamab; upon approval add teclistamab in a dual-targeting approach (RedirecTT-1 study)
Watch and wait regarding MDS-pCT (no excess blasts, sufficient peripheral blood counts)
Consider eligibility for clinical trial (may be complicated due to tMDS)
Consider local palliative radiotherapy depending on response and QoL impact

key_findings_quotes: multiple lines of therapy including two autologous stem cell transplants, KRD, Isa-Pom-Dex, and CAR-T with ide-cel, achieving CR in 2023
TP53 mutation (VAF 24%) in the CD138-negative fraction
dysplastic marrow changes, and detection of TP53 mutation with high VAF in the CD138- fraction, in line with therapy-related MDS
No excess of blasts was identified
Many anti-MM agents are myelotoxic limiting treatment options/possible dose intensity
Allogeneic stem cell transplantation is not considered feasible due to the patient's age
